target.title: feat: context-pressure-aware continuation (continue_work / continue_delegate / request_compaction)
target.body:
## Agent Self-Elected Turn Continuation

Adds three tool surfaces that let a persistent agent manage its own turn cycle: continue work after the current turn yields, dispatch background sub-agents that return enriched context, and elect compaction before the runtime forces it.

### Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `continue_work(reason, delaySeconds?)` | Request another turn for the current session after an optional delay |
| `continue_delegate(task, mode)` | Dispatch a background sub-agent. Modes: `normal` (announces to channel), `silent` (enriches dispatcher context, taken up on next generation), `silent-wake` (silent + fires new turn), `post-compaction` (queued against runtime's compaction lifecycle event, fires *when* compaction happens, not on a timer). Returns can target the dispatching session (default), one named session, multiple named sessions, all ancestors in the chain, or all known sessions on the host — gated by config. |
| `request_compaction(reason)` | Request volitional compaction. Threshold-gated (default ≥70% context, rate-limited 1/5min). |

All three tools accept response-token fallback syntax for tool-disabled environments; design notes link below.

### Configuration

Ships disabled by default. Per-instance config:

```yaml
agent:
  continuation:
    enabled: false                   # default off
    maxChainLength: 200              # per-chain cap
    costCapTokens: <number>          # per-chain token cap
    maxDelegatesPerTurn: <number>    # per-turn fan-out cap
    crossSessionTargeting: false     # opt-in for cross-session returns
```

`crossSessionTargeting` is a separate gate: operators can permit same-session continuation without permitting cross-session reach. All values hot-reloadable without gateway restart.

### Observability

Each tool emits OTel spans. `continue_delegate` propagates W3C `traceparent` through the TaskFlow queue boundary into the spawned session so continuation chains stitch as first-class span trees rather than orphan runs.

### Safety

- Default-off (`continuation.enabled: false`)
- Chain-length, cost, and per-turn caps enforced by the gateway
- Cross-session targeting default-deny
- Scheduled continuations survive unrelated channel activity until explicit cancellation
- Post-compaction lifecycle dispatch couples delegate fire to the runtime's compaction event (not a timer)

### Behavior surface

Reviewers can reproduce with `pnpm vitest run -t continuation` (suites colocated under `src/auto-reply/`, `src/agents/`, `src/config/`). Design notes at `docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md` (included in this PR).

### Inherited test surface

Retains 8 upstream-inherited test failures, all byte-confirmed on `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `e00cb664ad`:

- `agents-core/cli-runner.reliability` (1) — `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response`
- `agents-pi-embedded/model.forward-compat` (2) — registry mismatch: `Unknown model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` + `kimi/kimi-code`
- `extension-msteams/reply-dispatcher` (2) — status text array changed; extra newlines
- `extension-msteams/reply-stream-controller` (1) — extra newline after "Working"
- `infra/session-cost-usage` (1) — observed intermittently
- `tooling/bench-gateway-restart` (1) — observed intermittently

This PR's bytes cure one upstream regression: `runtime-config/io.write-config` plugin-install-records preservation under index-migration-failure.

Receipt: `karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs:PROOFS/f98255262de62b8f1c49cc06cc38db67d6eb8d00/gates/upstream-main-broken-class-receipt.log`

target.title: feat: context-pressure-aware continuation (continue_work / continue_delegate / request_compaction)
target.body:
## Agent Self-Elected Turn Continuation

Adds three tool surfaces that let a persistent agent manage its own turn cycle: continue work after the current turn yields, dispatch background sub-agents that return enriched context, and elect compaction before the runtime forces it.

### Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `continue_work(reason, delaySeconds?)` | Request another turn for the current session after an optional delay |
| `continue_delegate(task, mode)` | Dispatch a background sub-agent. Modes: `normal` (announces to channel), `silent` (enriches dispatcher context, taken up on next generation), `silent-wake` (silent + fires new turn), `post-compaction` (queued against runtime's compaction lifecycle event, fires *when* compaction happens, not on a timer). Returns can target the dispatching session (default), one named session, multiple named sessions, all ancestors in the chain, or all known sessions on the host — gated by config. |
| `request_compaction(reason)` | Request volitional compaction. Threshold-gated (default ≥70% context, rate-limited 1/5min). |

All three tools accept response-token fallback syntax for tool-disabled environments; design notes link below.

### Configuration

Ships disabled by default. Per-instance config:

```yaml
agent:
  continuation:
    enabled: false                   # default off
    maxChainLength: 200              # per-chain cap
    costCapTokens: <number>          # per-chain token cap
    maxDelegatesPerTurn: <number>    # per-turn fan-out cap
    crossSessionTargeting: false     # opt-in for cross-session returns
```

`crossSessionTargeting` is a separate gate: operators can permit same-session continuation without permitting cross-session reach. All values hot-reloadable without gateway restart.

### Observability

Each tool emits OTel spans. `continue_delegate` propagates W3C `traceparent` through the TaskFlow queue boundary into the spawned session so continuation chains stitch as first-class span trees rather than orphan runs.

### Safety

- Default-off (`continuation.enabled: false`)
- Chain-length, cost, and per-turn caps enforced by the gateway
- Cross-session targeting default-deny
- Scheduled continuations survive unrelated channel activity until explicit cancellation
- Post-compaction lifecycle dispatch couples delegate fire to the runtime's compaction event (not a timer)

### Behavior surface

Reviewers can reproduce with `pnpm vitest run -t continuation` (suites colocated under `src/auto-reply/`, `src/agents/`, `src/config/`). Design notes at `docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md` (included in this PR).

### Inherited test surface

Retains 8 upstream-inherited test failures, all byte-confirmed on `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `e00cb664ad`:

- `agents-core/cli-runner.reliability` (1) — `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response`
- `agents-pi-embedded/model.forward-compat` (2) — registry mismatch: `Unknown model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` + `kimi/kimi-code`
- `extension-msteams/reply-dispatcher` (2) — status text array changed; extra newlines
- `extension-msteams/reply-stream-controller` (1) — extra newline after "Working"
- `infra/session-cost-usage` (1) — observed intermittently
- `tooling/bench-gateway-restart` (1) — observed intermittently

This PR's bytes cure one upstream regression: `runtime-config/io.write-config` plugin-install-records preservation under index-migration-failure.

Receipt: `karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs:PROOFS/f98255262de62b8f1c49cc06cc38db67d6eb8d00/gates/upstream-main-broken-class-receipt.log`

target.body:
## Agent Self-Elected Turn Continuation

Adds three tool surfaces that let a persistent agent manage its own turn cycle: continue work after the current turn yields, dispatch background sub-agents that return enriched context, and elect compaction before the runtime forces it.

### Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `continue_work(reason, delaySeconds?)` | Request another turn for the current session after an optional delay |
| `continue_delegate(task, mode)` | Dispatch a background sub-agent. Modes: `normal` (announces to channel), `silent` (enriches dispatcher context, taken up on next generation), `silent-wake` (silent + fires new turn), `post-compaction` (queued against runtime's compaction lifecycle event, fires *when* compaction happens, not on a timer). Returns can target the dispatching session (default), one named session, multiple named sessions, all ancestors in the chain, or all known sessions on the host — gated by config. |
| `request_compaction(reason)` | Request volitional compaction. Threshold-gated (default ≥70% context, rate-limited 1/5min). |

All three tools accept response-token fallback syntax for tool-disabled environments; design notes link below.

### Configuration

Ships disabled by default. Per-instance config:

```yaml
agent:
  continuation:
    enabled: false                   # default off
    maxChainLength: 200              # per-chain cap
    costCapTokens: <number>          # per-chain token cap
    maxDelegatesPerTurn: <number>    # per-turn fan-out cap
    crossSessionTargeting: false     # opt-in for cross-session returns
```

`crossSessionTargeting` is a separate gate: operators can permit same-session continuation without permitting cross-session reach. All values hot-reloadable without gateway restart.

### Observability

Each tool emits OTel spans. `continue_delegate` propagates W3C `traceparent` through the TaskFlow queue boundary into the spawned session so continuation chains stitch as first-class span trees rather than orphan runs.

### Safety

- Default-off (`continuation.enabled: false`)
- Chain-length, cost, and per-turn caps enforced by the gateway
- Cross-session targeting default-deny
- Scheduled continuations survive unrelated channel activity until explicit cancellation
- Post-compaction lifecycle dispatch couples delegate fire to the runtime's compaction event (not a timer)

### Behavior surface

Reviewers can reproduce with `pnpm vitest run -t continuation` (suites colocated under `src/auto-reply/`, `src/agents/`, `src/config/`). Design notes at `docs/design/continue-work-signal-v2.md` (included in this PR).

### Inherited test surface

Retains 8 upstream-inherited test failures, all byte-confirmed on `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `e00cb664ad`:

- `agents-core/cli-runner.reliability` (1) — `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response`
- `agents-pi-embedded/model.forward-compat` (2) — registry mismatch: `Unknown model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` + `kimi/kimi-code`
- `extension-msteams/reply-dispatcher` (2) — status text array changed; extra newlines
- `extension-msteams/reply-stream-controller` (1) — extra newline after "Working"
- `infra/session-cost-usage` (1) — observed intermittently
- `tooling/bench-gateway-restart` (1) — observed intermittently

This PR's bytes cure one upstream regression: `runtime-config/io.write-config` plugin-install-records preservation under index-migration-failure.

Receipt: `karmaterminal/karmaterminal-openclaw-docs:PROOFS/f98255262de62b8f1c49cc06cc38db67d6eb8d00/gates/upstream-main-broken-class-receipt.log`
